name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether compression breakouts that initially fake one direction can produce a stronger re-expansion the other way once value is reaccepted and directional confirmation flips. The edge is not the first fake break; it is the reversal and re-expansion after the fake is revealed.
invalidation_condition: Abort if fakeout-and-reexpansion events remain too sparse, if reaccepted moves fail to expand enough after costs, or if TAO volatility only adds noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A compressed range breaks one direction, fails to gain acceptance, re-enters value, flips directional confirmation, and then re-expands in the opposite direction with a tradable move.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy markets generate fake breaks. On a volatile lane like TAO, the reversal after a failed compression break can be stronger than the initial fakeout if value is reaccepted cleanly.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because fakeouts and re-expansions are visible on a volatile lane without depending on lower-timeframe noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Fakeouts may occur, but if the reaccepted move cannot re-expand enough after entry the pattern becomes churn rather than edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger but re-expansion is too weak, reduce target ambition before tightening fakeout criteria.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest set of fakeout reversals with front-loaded exits carrying most expectancy and occasional expansion runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cleaner cross-lane show real density and positive post-cost re-expansion after failed compression breaks.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or fakeout reversals that repeatedly fail to re-expand.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; compression, fakeout, reacceptance, and directional flip are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad fakeout-and-return logic rather than exact breakout candle dependence

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for compression fakeout re-expansion in the live basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Fakeout reversals occur often enough and re-expand after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The pattern is too sparse or re-expansion never materializes.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Failed compression breaks that reverse and produce a cleaner second move in the opposite direction.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same fakeout-and-reexpansion grammar survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the same re-expansion pattern with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only works on TAO-style volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner fakeout reversals than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same fakeout-return grammar survives on a faster adjacent lane inside the basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h preserves the fakeout-reexpansion shape without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The faster lane becomes too noisy or overtrades without edge.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller fakeout re-expansions than 4h.
